title: Corruption
questions: Which areas do you think are the most corrupt (a) in your country (b) in other countries? E.g. Politics, education, construction, health, agriculture, the arms industry, transport, etc.
What are the effects of corruption on society?
Who is more to blame - the person who offers a bribe or the person who takes it?
Can corruption ever benefit a country?
How does corruption affect (a) people's lives in general (b) your life in particular?
What can be done to reduce or stop corruption?
How should corrupt officials be dealt with when they are found out?
Why are some countries more corrupt than others?
Is corruption more prevalent in democracies or autocracies? Why is this so?
What effect will globalisation have on corruption, do you think?
Is it OK to bribe children? Did your parents bribe you as a child?/Do you bribe your children?
Can corruption ever be wiped out or is it part of human nature?
